TextENGNew: ``I'm sorry... Yuusuke.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2096. I swore that I'd never make this sort of report... but what a wretched situation this is.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2097. ...If you hate me for this, I don't mind.``
After setting the flowers in the flower stand, lighting a stick of incense, and clapping my hands together in silence... I bent down on my knees and deeply bowed down in front of the grave.
Of course, there was no reply.
In front of me was nothing but a stone with letters etched into it, and the remains of a departed man stored beneath it.
Nevertheless, as someone who contributed to him and his wife's deaths, I could not think of any better way to apologize.
Maeno was right beside me, kneeling on the stone pavement and leaning forward, as large tears came flowing down his face.
He resembled he was in pain even before we got here.
...Seeing the tombstone right in front of him must have really gotten to him.
But... I do not think there is anything embarrassing about a full-grown man doing so in this situation.
In fact... I was envious of his youthful propensity to express his emotions so honestly.

``...@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2099.Yuusuke... and Nobumi-san.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2100. Investigation headquarters shut down the case yesterday... without ever finding the truth of this incident.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2101. I'm... truly sorry.``
A dull thud rang out.
...Looking down, Maeno was pounding his fist on the ground in front of him.
...Five years ago.
Maeno was appointed as a young executive candidate for the career group, and had been serving as a police officer since he was assigned to the Public Security bureau.
But since he had no actual investigation experience and only ever worked a desk job, Minai was assigned to be his tutor on account of his exceptional track record.
``...If he starts getting too brazen, give him a nice kick in the shin.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2103. I'm relying on you.``
``Hahaha! Understood.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_minaiF2003. But he's still young, so I think a little overconfidence is a sign of a promising future.``
Having uttered those words, Yuusuke did not pay much heed to Maeno's audacious demeanor.
In fact, he was willing to play along with him, serving as a cushion to eliminate conflicts with others.

As a matter of fact... I was planning to break down Maeno's aggressive overconfidence and retrain him... but it seems Yuusuke's educational policy was the best way to handle a competitive spirit like his.

Maeno had grown since then, polishing his skills and his senses... He became motivated and capable enough to act as a squad leader in the Shirakaba mountain village siege incident just a few years later.
He was not raised with a limited outlook, so he was able to achieve great success through his unconventional thoughts and courage...

(Maeno had a strong tendency to stand up and oppose the things he did not agree with... but he felt lonely in situations where he was misunderstood.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2105. Yuusuke recognized that.)

And Maeno, too, had full confidence in the open-hearted Yuusuke. Even after they stopped being boss and subordinate to one another, Maeno never stopped addressing him as ``Minai-san`` and always spoke to him in honorific language.
He always listened to him, and respected any comments and suggestions Yuusuke gave him.
For a self-confident man like Maeno, that was a rare thing...
``I'm sorry... Minai-san...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2289. I'm sorry, I'm sorry...!!``
...Maeno continued to apologize, rubbing his head on the ground and crying nonstop.
...This was the second time I would seen him this deeply in tears.
The first was the night of the fire at Yuusuke's house... Or rather, I should say the ``arson`` that claimed his life.
...At first, nobody noticed the fierce rage he felt.
In front of the other attendees at the funeral... Maeno remained expressionless, just staring at the picture of the deceased on the stage.
Nevertheless, after putting Minai's daughters to bed in another room and heading back to the hall to watch over the deceased in his final rest... along the way,
I felt like I could hear someone moaning from the woods behind the garden... so I gently stepped down from the veranda and headed into the garden.
And then...
``Damn it... damn iiiit!!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2292. I'll kill you... Whoever you are that targeted Minai-san, I swear I'll kill you with my own two hands!!``
Maeno was pushing his forehead against a tree trunk, letting out a beastly roar filled with anger and sobs.
The burning flames emanating from his sharp glare, and the look on his face as he roared like he was breathing fire... was something I will never forget.
...Subsequently, Maeno became so absorbed in searching for the culprit that he forgot to even eat and sleep.
Ever since an investigation headquarters was established within Public Security, even people who knew him in the past could not believe their eyes when they saw him.
``I swear, I won't hand this case over to anyone else...!``
Maeno went off to gather information on his own after declaring that, without relying on the other investigators.
Even though he had several other cases he was investigating at the same time, he continued working like it was no burden at all.
However... the primary mission of Public Security is to protect national security and maintain public order.
Not to investigate the criminals behind murder cases.

And what's more, upper management doesn't take kindly to field workers running on obsessions like avenging their colleagues... so finally, notice was passed down transferring the manager of investigation headquarters to the First Investigation Division.
``Huuh?!``
...The screams and shouts of the investigators who heard it were thunderous enough to shake the large conference room.
There was nothing I could do to comfort them, so I had no choice but to overbearingly cut them off and then take my leave.
Nevertheless, the flames in their hearts did not fade so easily... they continued to smolder.
Especially Maeno. The young investigators even appealed to upper management yelling threateningly, and they were about to hit my office.
``Commissioner Yamaoki, I can't accept this!``

``Minai-san was investigating something connected to the Lowell scandal, and was caught up in a fire right before he could make his report!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2296. That means there's a high likelihood he was killed in an organized plot related to that scandal!``
``In which case, isn't it perfectly natural for us to investigate it?!``
````Yeah, that is right!!````
``...@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2107.Indeed, that may be so.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2108. However... the First Division has already declared that the fire was an accident.``
``Thus, unless you can find enough evidence to overturn that decision, you can't claim it's part of some conspiracy.``
``That's why!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2299. I'm certain we'll be able to find evidence of that!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2300. So, please... Commissioner!``
``A little longer... just a little longer!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2302. Let us continue our investigation!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2303. I'm begging you!!``
...Even so, upper management never heard their wishes.
And the conclusion the First Division reached was based on testimony from firefighters... ``unforeseen accidental fire.``
Sparks from a faulty electrical socket caught a nearby curtain on fire, which was the initial source of the fire... or so they say.
``Maeno...``
``It's impossible... Impossible!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2305. Even if the fire was an accident, how could Minai-san burn to death without noticing it?``
``It's impossible to think the body would be found in the bedroom without showing any signs of attempting to escape!!``
``...Indeed.``
Out of all my subordinates, Yuusuke was especially good at assessing the situation... His technique of acting carefree while observing his target was truly genius.
Even when we went out drinking like usual, on the way home he happened to notice that one of the people we walked past had a pistol. He was always alert and on guard.
How could he have slept through a fire without noticing it and burned to death?
...Needless to say, Maeno did not buy it either.
And when the investigation into the cause of death was shut off by upper management... I was fully convinced.
The people who killed Yuusuke and his wife... were probably putting pressure on them, too...
(In which case, I wished to at least have him declared killed in the line of duty.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2114. For the sake of the daughters he left behind...)
Pension and severance allowances are provided for a deputy inspector in case of accidental death when off duty.
But even considering his years of service, I do not think that would amount to much.
Meanwhile, someone pronounced killed in the line of duty is automatically promoted two ranks and declared a superintendent.
On top of that, additional payments are prepared for the victim's bereaved family... it would've allowed the two of them to live comfortably.
Nevertheless, the personnel department insisted that he would not be classified as killed in the line of duty...
I clenched my teeth and squeezed my fists so tight my nails dug into my palms.
Yuusuke was acting on my orders, pursuing something that seemed to be connected to the Lowell incident.
In fact, the very day after his death... I was scheduled to hear an interim report from him.
In other words, I cannot help but think that the murder of the Minai couple was an attempt to obstruct that, masquerading as an accident... No, what else could it be?!

(...Yuusuke.@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2117. Was the enemy you were pursuing that powerful?@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2118. If that is the case... what on earth can we do?@k@vS20/30/VTK2_yamaoki2119. How can we stand up to them...?!)
The justice of the police is absolute.
...We've stood up to dangerous situations and vicious criminals, backed by that pride and belief.
But if such an ``absolute,`` unshakable evil entity were to appear... what should we do?
Look the other way?
Give up?
